deploy.ci: .github/workflows/deploy.yml runs npm ci, npm test, npm run build on node 22 and publishes dist to GitHub Pages
deploy.netlify: retired and dead; every historical Netlify site and the npx netlify deploy route is out of the deploy path and must not be used
deploy.approvalRule: no push or deploy without Robbie's explicit approval after local verification is summarized

contentEpochNote: Launch L1 0.99.2 shipped the content epoch as a real per-run runtime stamp (route-run.contentEpoch, save v8), separate from mapSchema (generator structure) and saveSchema (serialized shape). genMap resolves treasure pools, district power, and the enchant pool through epoch tables; the current epoch computes live so today's maps stay byte-identical. A future content or balance change bumps CONTENT_EPOCH and freezes the epoch it leaves into EPOCH_TABLES, so a supported active run regenerates its own epoch's map instead of being retired. readRouteSave retires only on a mapSchema mismatch.

activeProject.name: Launch L2
activeProject.outcome: Calibrated challenge: tune the difficulty the Launch L1 smoke surfaced and remove dead hero and Omen pairings, without reopening settled combat, targeting, economy, map, or save semantics. Work stays isolated, attributable, and exact once.

reservations.scope: Hero and Omen compatibility. A fresh random run filters the unlocked eligible Omen pool before the single deterministic draw so the Apothecary never opens under Blood Moon, whose healing, regeneration, and lifesteal lockout nullifies the entire heal kit. No redraw loop and no extra rng draw, so every other hero and Omen selection stays byte-identical; explicit replay overrides, active or historical Apothecary plus Blood Moon runs, and the simulator's explicit Apothecary plus Blood Moon negative-control cell are all preserved. No combat, difficulty constant, src/data.js, targeting, economy, map, or save change.

launchL2Base.note: The canonical Launch L2 base is the closed Launch L1 head at product baseline 6d3ed77 (docs: log the After Midnight storm wall as a Launch L2 difficulty input), a verified ancestor of the launch-safe-ground HEAD. Launch L1 is CLOSED (see launchL1Status): all three versions integrated and deployed and the gate signed, and the After Midnight storm-wall difficulty input (see launchL2Inputs) is recorded on this base for the calibrated-challenge work. No combat, targeting, economy, map, or save semantics change to reach this base; the reconciliation is canonical-state only.

launchL1Status: CLOSED. All three Launch L1 versions (0.99.1 coordination, 0.99.2 content epoch, 0.99.3 route decisions) are integrated and deployed to https://platosd.com (origin/main). Codex signed the technical gate. Robbie's on-device smoke passed (full game playthrough, Long mode, Moneylender + Guild Charter, district 6 Ifrit loss, 4 gate retries, all checkpoints and decisions settled exactly once). Launch L1 exit criteria met. The closure carries forward unchanged as the Launch L2 base (see launchL2Base).

launchL2Inputs.source: Robbie on-device smoke 2026-07-18, run r1slbuqc at platosd.com
launchL2Inputs.owner: difficulty worker (0.101.0), Launch L2 calibrated challenge
launchL2Inputs.signal: Sharp difficulty cliff at the After Midnight bosses. The player cleared districts 1 through 4 at or near full health, then lost five straight attempts to the district 6 Ifrit boss and never dropped it below about 247 of 1537 health.
launchL2Inputs.rootCause: Storm timing, not board strength. stormAt was 16s but the late fights ran 24 to 26s, so storm dealt 378 to 550 unattributed damage per attempt and dominated the loss. The board (Silver icy Torch, Silver Sword, Gold swift Fire Bomb, Gold stout March, Silver venomous Writ, Salt Ward at tier 4) was adequate through the front half but could not close before the storm buried it.
launchL2Inputs.lever: The After Midnight power multiplier (2.05 at d6) stacked on top of the fixed storm arrival is the specific spike. The front half at power 1 gave no such pressure. Consider easing storm timing or the After Midnight multiplier in the back half, or telegraphing the storm clock so players build to beat it.
launchL2Inputs.relatedFinding: Reinforces the earlier real-playtest note that spikes come from the offensive-opening plus Dragon Gate pattern rather than from tier cost.

reservationLedger.verification: npm ci restored 52 packages from the committed lockfile with the lockfile unchanged; npm test passed 507 of 507 (13 new coordination tests); npm run build succeeded on vite 6.4.3.
reservationLedger.scope: Canonical coordination state, reservation validator, explicit staging in ship.js, and doc reconciliation (AGENTS.md tracked and reduced, CLAUDE.md and ROADMAP.md pointers, roadmap refinements R1 and R2).

reservationLedger.verification: npm test 515 of 515 (8 new content-epoch tests; byte-identical map and combat parity goldens hold); npm run build green; Playwright resume crash-recovery matrix 34 of 34 across both viewports; layout 26 of 28 (2 pre-existing stale Omen-count failures from 0.98.0, unrelated).
reservationLedger.scope: Content epoch decoupled from map retirement: CONTENT_EPOCH separate from MAP_VERSION and ROUTE_SAVE_VERSION, epoch-aware genMap, save v8 with migrateV7toV8, and readRouteSave retiring only on a map-structure mismatch.

reservationLedger.verification: Claude re-verified after cherry-pick: npm test 523 of 523; npm run build green; Playwright route-decisions recovery 2 of 2 and inherited resume matrix 34 of 34 across both viewports. Codex reported the same on its worktree. Robbie's on-device smoke passed: full playthrough Long mode with Moneylender+Charter, reached district 6, lost to Ifrit after 4 retries, all checkpoint and decision transactions settled exactly once. The seam changes no engine, data, map, or route-persistence file, so there is no combat, balance, or targeting change.
reservationLedger.scope: Behavior-preserving route decision transaction seam (src/route-decisions.js): a durable receipt freezes offers before display; commitRouteDecision validates before mutation, applies payment or reward, records the metric, advances the route, and marks the receipt in one synchronous transaction, with a duplicate guard for exact-once settlement across reload.

namespace.ruling: Launch L1 through Launch L5 with the descriptive tokens launch-safe-ground, launch-calibrated-challenge, launch-market-agency, launch-route-choice-equity, and launch-release-experience. Historical content R8 keeps its name; no R8 code or test identifier is renamed. Never abbreviate a project to a bare L1 through L5, because the Lantern ladder already uses L1 through L10.

ownerSplit.ruling: Claude leads and integrates Launch L1 and implements 0.99.1 and 0.99.2. Codex reviews the frozen 0.99.2 integration, implements 0.99.3, and signs the Launch L1 exit gate. Codex leads Launch L3 and Launch L4. The difficulty worker owns 0.101.0. Claude leads Launch L5.

targetingReopen.ruling: Keep settled. No bulwark taunt or flying immunity reopen before 1.0. A later veto requires new reservations and renumbering.

signatureModel.ruling: Keep Model B. Unlocking a hero atomically unlocks both signature wares, and signatures stay outside the global Almanac chase. Fix presentation debt in 0.108.0. A veto requires a separately reserved progression migration version and renumbering.

displayName.ruling: Pending. Robbie decides, and it is due before 0.111.0 native metadata. Silence does not resolve it.

deploy.ruling: GitHub Pages only at platosd.com via git push origin main. Robbie approves each push at every deploy gate.

reviewRulings.acceptance: Claude's frozen-target acceptance review returned ACCEPT for all five projects on 2026-07-18 against the roadmap at commit 15e8995.
reviewRulings.refinementR1: The full local dev dependency tree was absent, not only vite. It is restored with npm ci from the committed lockfile without changing the lockfile. Folded into the Launch L1 doc reconciliation.
reviewRulings.refinementR2: Bare L1 through L5 is forbidden because the Lantern ladder uses L1 through L10. Use the full Launch L labels in prose and the descriptive tokens in all machine readable state. Folded into the Launch L1 doc reconciliation.
